Summary: Successful login with valid email and password
Description: Verify a registered user can log in using valid email and password credentials.
Precondition: User has a registered VWO account. Browser open at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid registered email
3. Enter correct password
4. Click login/submit button
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the personalized VWO dashboard.
PRD Source: PRD §Functional Requirements – Login Process

Summary: Login fails with invalid password
Description: Verify login is rejected when correct email is used with a wrong password.
Precondition: User has a registered VWO account. Browser at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid registered email
3. Enter incorrect password
4. Click login/submit button
Expected Result: Authentication fails. Clear actionable error message is displayed. User remains on login page.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Error Handling

Summary: Login fails with unregistered email address
Description: Verify login is rejected when an email not associated with any account is entered.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter unregistered email
3. Enter any password
4. Click login/submit button
Expected Result: Authentication fails. Clear actionable error message is displayed.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Error Handling

Summary: Login blocked with empty email field
Description: Verify form submission is blocked when the email field is empty.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Leave email field empty
3. Enter any value in password
4. Attempt to submit form
Expected Result: Form submission is blocked. Validation error displayed on email field.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Real-time Validation

Summary: Login blocked with empty password field
Description: Verify form submission is blocked when the password field is empty.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid email
3. Leave password field empty
4. Attempt to submit form
Expected Result: Form submission is blocked. Validation error displayed on password field.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Real-time Validation

Summary: Email field validates format on blur
Description: Verify real-time email format validation triggers when focus leaves the email field.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Click into email field
3. Enter invalid format (e.g. userATdomain)
4. Click outside email field (blur event)
Expected Result: Inline validation error indicating invalid email format is displayed immediately after blur.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Real-time Validation, Email Format Verification

Summary: Email field accepts valid email format
Description: Verify a properly formatted email passes format validation.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid email (e.g. user@domain.com)
3. Click outside the email field
Expected Result: No validation error displayed for the email field.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Email Format Verification

Summary: Remember Me checkbox is present on login page
Description: Verify a Remember Me checkbox exists on the login form.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect login form for a Remember Me checkbox
Expected Result: A Remember Me checkbox is visible on the login form.
PRD Source: PRD §Existing Features – Remember Me Functionality

Summary: Remember Me persists session across browser restart
Description: Verify checking Remember Me results in a persistent login session.
Precondition: User has valid credentials. Browser at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid credentials
3. Check Remember Me checkbox
4. Log in successfully
5. Close and reopen the browser
6. Navigate to app.vwo.com
Expected Result: User session is persisted; user is not required to log in again.
PRD Source: PRD §Remember Me Functionality; §Session Management

Summary: Forgot Password link is present on login page
Description: Verify a Forgot Password link is accessible from the login page.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect login page for a Forgot Password link
Expected Result: A Forgot Password link or button is visible on the login page.
PRD Source: PRD §Password Management – Forgot Password Flow

Summary: Forgot Password flow accepts registered email
Description: Verify submitting a registered email through Forgot Password initiates the reset process.
Precondition: User has a registered VWO account. On Forgot Password page.
Test Steps:
1. Click Forgot Password on login page
2. Enter a registered email address
3. Submit the form
Expected Result: System acknowledges the request with a confirmation message. Secure password reset email is triggered.
PRD Source: PRD §Password Management – Forgot Password Flow, Password Recovery

Summary: Forgot Password flow with unregistered email
Description: Verify system behavior when an unregistered email is submitted in Forgot Password.
Test Steps:
1. Click Forgot Password on login page
2. Enter an unregistered email address
3. Submit form
Expected Result: System responds with a clear message. No account existence information is leaked.
PRD Source: PRD §Password Management – Forgot Password Flow; §Error Handling
Confidence: Verified + Inference (low confidence: account non-disclosure not explicit in PRD)

Summary: Free trial signup CTA is present on login page
Description: Verify a free trial registration call-to-action is accessible from the login page.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect page for registration or free trial link
Expected Result: A clearly visible free trial / registration CTA is present on the login page.
PRD Source: PRD §Existing Features – Account Registration Link

Summary: Login page is served over HTTPS
Description: Verify login page enforces HTTPS / SSL-TLS encryption.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect browser address bar and SSL certificate
Expected Result: URL begins with https://. Valid SSL/TLS certificate is present. No mixed-content warnings shown.
PRD Source: PRD §Security Specifications – HTTPS Enforcement

Summary: Rate limiting applied after repeated failed login attempts
Description: Verify system applies rate limiting after multiple consecutive failed login attempts.
Precondition: User account exists. Browser at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid email with incorrect password
3. Repeat 5-10 times rapidly in quick succession
Expected Result: System applies rate limiting or temporarily blocks further login attempts. Relevant error or warning message is displayed.
PRD Source: PRD §Security Specifications – Rate Limiting
Confidence: Verified (exact lockout threshold not specified in PRD)

Summary: Password field masks input characters
Description: Verify characters entered in the password field are masked by default.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Click into password field
3. Type any characters
Expected Result: Entered characters are displayed as masked symbols (dots or asterisks).
PRD Source: PRD §Security Specifications – Data Protection

Summary: Login page loads within 2 seconds on standard connection
Description: Verify login page meets the defined sub-2-second load time performance target.
Precondition: Standard internet connection. Browser cache cleared.
Test Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools Network tab
2. Navigate to app.vwo.com
3. Record total page load time
Expected Result: Total page load time is under 2 seconds on a standard connection.
PRD Source: PRD §Performance Requirements – Page Load Speed

Summary: All form fields are keyboard navigable
Description: Verify users can navigate all login form fields using keyboard only.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Do not use mouse
3. Use Tab key to navigate through all elements
4. Use Enter/Space to interact with buttons and checkboxes
Expected Result: All interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard alone.
PRD Source: PRD §Accessibility Features – Keyboard Navigation

Summary: Login form fields have ARIA labels for screen reader support
Description: Verify form inputs include ARIA labels or associated label elements.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Accessibility audit tool available.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Run accessibility audit (axe or Lighthouse)
3. Inspect ARIA attributes on email and password fields
Expected Result: Email and password fields have descriptive ARIA labels. No critical ARIA violations reported.
PRD Source: PRD §Accessibility Features – Screen Reader Support, ARIA labels

Summary: Auto-focus applied to first input field on page load
Description: Verify keyboard focus is automatically placed on the first input field when the login page loads.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Observe which element has focus immediately after page load (without clicking)
Expected Result: Email input field has keyboard focus immediately on page load without any user interaction.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Auto-focus

Summary: Login page is responsive on mobile viewport
Description: Verify login form renders correctly on a mobile screen size.
Precondition: Browser DevTools mobile emulation or physical mobile device.
Test Steps:
1. Open app.vwo.com at 375px width (DevTools or physical mobile)
2. Inspect layout, input fields, and buttons
Expected Result: All form elements are visible, usable, and not clipped or overflowing. Touch targets are adequately sized.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Responsive Design

Summary: Email input triggers email-optimized keyboard on mobile
Description: Verify the email input field activates an email-optimized keyboard on mobile.
Precondition: Physical mobile device or browser mobile emulation at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Open app.vwo.com on a mobile device
2. Tap the email input field
Expected Result: Device displays an email-type keyboard (@ and .com keys prominent).
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Email Format Verification (specialized mobile keyboards)

Summary: Light and Dark Mode option present on login page
Description: Verify login page presents a theme toggle or announcement for Light and Dark Mode.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect page for theme toggle or Light/Dark Mode banner
Expected Result: A UI element or product announcement related to Light and Dark Mode is visible on the login page.
PRD Source: PRD §Existing Features – Product Announcements (Light and Dark Mode)

Summary: Loading state displayed during authentication processing
Description: Verify user receives a loading state or indicator during authentication processing.
Precondition: User has valid credentials. Browser at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid credentials
3. Submit login form
4. Observe UI feedback during authentication
Expected Result: A loading state or progress indicator is shown during authentication. User is then redirected to the VWO dashboard.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Loading States; §Error Recovery Flow – Success Confirmation
Confidence: Verified + Inference (low confidence: indicator type not specified in PRD)

Summary: SSO login option accessible for enterprise users
Description: Verify an SSO login option is present for enterprise account users.
Precondition: Enterprise SSO account configured. Browser at app.vwo.com.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Look for SSO or enterprise login option
3. Initiate SSO authentication flow
Expected Result: SSO login option is accessible. Initiating SSO redirects user to configured identity provider.
PRD Source: PRD §Authentication System – SSO; §Third-Party Services – Enterprise SSO

Summary: Login page meets WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast requirements
Description: Verify text and interactive elements meet minimum WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio of 4.5:1.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Color contrast analysis tool available.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Run WCAG color contrast check (Lighthouse / axe / Colour Contrast Analyser)
3. Review results for all text and interactive elements
Expected Result: All text elements meet a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 as required by WCAG 2.1 AA.
PRD Source: PRD §Accessibility Standards – WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

Summary: Email field has a visible label
Description: Verify that the email input field has a visible, descriptive label on the login form.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect the login form
3. Identify the label associated with the email input field
Expected Result: A visible label is present and clearly identifies the email input field.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Clickable Labels; §Accessibility – Screen Reader Support

Summary: Password field has a visible label
Description: Verify that the password input field has a visible, descriptive label on the login form.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect the login form
3. Identify the label associated with the password input field
Expected Result: A visible label is present and clearly identifies the password input field.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Clickable Labels; §Accessibility – Screen Reader Support

Summary: Clicking the email field label focuses the email input
Description: Verify that clicking the email field label transfers focus to the email input (clickable label behaviour).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Click directly on the email field label text (not the input box itself)
Expected Result: Keyboard focus moves to the email input field. Cursor is active in the email field.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Clickable Labels

Summary: Clicking the password field label focuses the password input
Description: Verify that clicking the password field label transfers focus to the password input.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Click directly on the password field label text (not the input box itself)
Expected Result: Keyboard focus moves to the password input field. Cursor is active in the password field.
PRD Source: PRD §Interface Design – Clickable Labels

Summary: Form field labels are programmatically associated with their inputs
Description: Verify that labels are correctly associated with their inputs via for/id or ARIA (not just visually co-located).
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Browser DevTools or accessibility audit tool available.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Inspect HTML of email and password fields
3. Verify <label for> matches input id, OR aria-labelledby / aria-label is set
Expected Result: Each input field has a programmatically associated label. Accessibility audit reports no missing label errors.
PRD Source: PRD §Accessibility Features – Clickable Labels, Screen Reader Support, ARIA labels

Summary: Error message is displayed inline near the relevant field
Description: Verify that validation error messages appear inline adjacent to the field that caused the error.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter an invalid email format
3. Attempt to submit or blur the field
4. Observe location of error message
Expected Result: Error message is displayed inline, adjacent to the email field — not in a generic banner far from the field.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Error Handling (clear actionable error messages)

Summary: Error message text is clear and actionable
Description: Verify error message copy tells the user what went wrong and how to fix it.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter invalid email format and submit
3. Read the error message text
Expected Result: Error message text is descriptive and actionable (e.g. indicates the field is invalid and what is expected). Does not display a generic or cryptic code.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Error Handling (clear actionable error messages)

Summary: Error message clears when user corrects invalid email input
Description: Verify inline error on email field is dismissed once the user enters a valid email.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Email validation error is currently displayed.
Test Steps:
1. Trigger email validation error (invalid format + blur)
2. Correct the email to a valid format
3. Observe whether error message disappears
Expected Result: Error message is cleared / dismissed once a valid email format is entered.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Real-time Validation, Error Handling

Summary: Error message displayed on failed login is visible and readable
Description: Verify that authentication failure error messages are visible, styled, and readable.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Enter valid email with wrong password
3. Submit
4. Inspect error message visibility (color, contrast, placement)
Expected Result: Error message is visible, has sufficient color contrast, and is not hidden or overlapped by other UI elements.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Error Handling; §Accessibility – WCAG 2.1 AA

Summary: Multiple field errors shown simultaneously
Description: Verify that errors for multiple empty or invalid fields are all shown at the same time on form submission.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to app.vwo.com
2. Leave both email and password fields empty
3. Attempt to submit form
Expected Result: Validation errors for both the email field and password field are displayed simultaneously.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Real-time Validation, Error Handling

Summary: Password strength indicator is displayed during password entry
Description: Verify that a visual password strength indicator appears as the user types in the password field.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com on a page where password entry is applicable (e.g. registration or reset).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the password entry page (registration or reset flow)
2. Click into the password field
3. Begin typing characters
4. Observe UI for a strength indicator
Expected Result: A visual password strength indicator (e.g. weak / medium / strong bar or label) is displayed as the user types.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Password Strength Indicators

Summary: Password strength indicator updates dynamically as password changes
Description: Verify the strength indicator reflects changes in real time as the user modifies the password.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Password entry page.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the password entry page
2. Type a short/simple password (e.g. 'abc')
3. Observe strength indicator
4. Extend password with numbers and symbols
5. Observe indicator again
Expected Result: Password strength indicator updates dynamically — reflects lower strength for simple input and higher strength as complexity increases.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Password Strength Indicators

Summary: Password not meeting complexity requirements is rejected
Description: Verify that a password not meeting enforced complexity requirements cannot be submitted.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Password creation page (registration or reset).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to password creation page
2. Enter a password that does not meet complexity requirements (e.g. only lowercase letters)
3. Attempt to submit
Expected Result: Form submission is blocked. An error message is displayed indicating the password does not meet the required complexity standards.
PRD Source: PRD §Authentication System – Password Requirements (enforced security standards for password complexity)

Summary: Password requirements are communicated to the user
Description: Verify that password complexity requirements are displayed to the user before or during password entry.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Password creation page (registration or reset).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to password creation page
2. Click into or interact with the password field
3. Inspect UI for any displayed password requirements or hints
Expected Result: Password requirements (e.g. minimum length, required character types) are shown to the user as guidance text or tooltip.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Password Strength Indicators; §Password Requirements

Summary: Password strength indicator provides visual feedback distinguishing weak from strong passwords
Description: Verify the visual feedback clearly differentiates between weak, medium, and strong passwords.
Precondition: Browser at app.vwo.com. Password creation page.
Test Steps:
1. Enter a weak password (e.g. single word, no numbers)
2. Note strength indicator state
3. Enter a strong password (mixed case, numbers, symbols)
4. Note strength indicator state
Expected Result: Strength indicator clearly shows a different visual state (e.g. colour, label, or fill level) for a weak versus strong password.
PRD Source: PRD §User Input Validation – Password Strength Indicators (visual feedback for password requirements and strength)
